zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏多
zh.content:
当前AVAX/USD维持窄幅震荡结构，价格围绕6.30一线整理，短期趋势略偏多但动能有限。整体仍处盘整上沿，方向等待突破确认

MACD柱体维持轻微正值，短线多头动能仍在延续，但信号线缺失使趋势确认度下降，需观察背离情况整体指标呈弱多格局，震荡概率较高

下方支撑位于6.28及6.25区域，上方阻力集中在6.33与6.40附近，区间结构清晰短期维持箱体整理等待方向选择

操作建议以区间交易为主，低位接近支撑可分批试多，高位接近阻力逐步止盈，控制杠杆。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Avalanche is currently trading in a tight consolidation band, showing very low volatility after a marginal 24-hour change. Price action is compressing between roughly 6.285 and 6.3338, indicating equilibrium between buyers and sellers. Despite the lack of directional momentum, the structure leans slightly constructive as price holds above recent intraday lows. The broader short-term tone suggests accumulation rather than distribution, with market participants waiting for a breakout trigger to define the next directional move.

From an indicator standpoint, the picture is cautiously bullish. Price holding above the 20-period simple moving average at 6.28326 signals that short-term trend support is intact. The MACD reading at 0.025292 suggests mild positive momentum, though the absence of a signal line reading limits confirmation strength. With RSI unavailable, momentum assessment relies more heavily on price structure and moving average positioning, both of which currently favor a neutral-to-slightly bullish bias.

Key support is clearly established at the SMA20 near 6.283, closely aligned with the session low at 6.285, forming a strong near-term demand zone. A break below this area could expose downside pressure toward 6.25 and potentially 6.20 if selling accelerates. On the upside, immediate resistance sits at 6.3338, with a stronger ceiling forming near 6.35–6.40 where previous supply has emerged and price rejection is more likely.

Trading conditions favor patience and range-based strategies until a breakout occurs. Traders may consider buying near support with tight risk controls, targeting the upper boundary of the range. Alternatively, a breakout above 6.34 with volume expansion could signal continuation toward 6.40+. Conversely, a sustained close below 6.28 would weaken the structure and shift bias toward short-term downside continuation.
